text: Explain why small language models benefit from on-policy distillation.
completion: They learn from teacher feedback on their own rollouts, reducing train-test mismatch.

text: Summarize the difference between forward KL and reverse KL.
completion: Forward KL rewards covering teacher probability mass; reverse KL favors sharp mode seeking.

text: Write a concise checklist for debugging a CUDA training step.
completion: Check shapes, device residency, finite loss, gradient norms, optimizer state, and memory.

text: What makes a benchmark result trustworthy?
completion: Matched controls, repeated runs, low variance, clear hardware, and wall-clock framing.

text: Describe how a frozen teacher can guide a perturbed student model.
completion: The teacher scores outputs while gradients update only the student toward similar logits.

text: Give a short example of a prompt that tests arithmetic reasoning.
completion: If twelve apples are split among three boxes equally, how many per box?

text: List three signs that a training run is diverging.
completion: Loss spikes, gradients become non-finite, and held-out metrics collapse quickly.

text: Explain token-level supervision in one paragraph.
completion: Token-level supervision trains each next-token prediction against explicit target tokens.

text: How should an engineer decide whether to keep or kill an optimization?
completion: Keep it only if matched A/B runs show a meaningful wall-clock improvement.

text: Write a compact note about GPU memory leak monitoring.
completion: Record before, peak, and after memory; stop if usage grows each iteration.

text: Why is exact-token overlap sometimes too coarse as an eval metric?
completion: It misses probability improvements when the top token does not change yet.

text: Compare greedy decoding with sampled rollouts for distillation.
completion: Greedy rollouts are deterministic; sampled rollouts expose more modes but add variance.

text: What is the role of held-out prompts in an OPD experiment?
completion: They reveal whether training generalizes beyond the prompts used for updates.

text: Explain why matched controls matter in performance work.
completion: They isolate the changed variable so timing deltas can be attributed honestly.

text: Give a one-sentence definition of runtime-integrated training.
completion: Runtime-integrated training reuses serving kernels and schedulers inside the training loop.

text: What should a user check before running a Qwen tokenizer benchmark?
completion: Use the matching tokenizer.json, prompt set, max tokens, and deterministic seed.

text: Describe a safe default learning rate for a near-checkpoint student.
completion: Start very small, such as 1e-7 for full weights or tuned LoRA rates.

text: Why can a loss curve improve while exact overlap stays flat?
completion: Probabilities can move toward the teacher before argmax token identities change.

text: Write a short warning about padding prompts without an attention mask.
completion: Padding without a correct mask lets pad tokens affect attention and corrupt logits.

text: Explain why a real text prompt set is better than hand-picked token IDs.
completion: Real prompts preserve tokenizer distribution, semantics, and evaluation relevance.
